description:
A network performance engineer is investigating suboptimal throughput and needs to audit TCP options negotiated during connection setup. Analyze all initial TCP SYN packets (SYN flag set, ACK flag not set) in /home/ga/Documents/captures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng and produce two output files.

1. A CSV report at /home/ga/Documents/tcp_options_report.csv including a header row with the following columns:
- frame_number: the packet frame number
- src_ip: source IP address
- dst_ip: destination IP address
- src_port: source TCP port
- dst_port: destination TCP port
- mss: TCP Maximum Segment Size value (empty string if not present)
- window_scale: TCP Window Scale shift count (empty string if not present)
- sack_permitted: 'yes' if SACK Permitted option present, 'no' otherwise
- timestamp_present: 'yes' if TCP Timestamps option present, 'no' otherwise
- initial_window_size: the TCP window size value from the SYN header

2. A summary text file at /home/ga/Documents/tcp_options_summary.txt with exactly these labeled lines:
- total_syn_packets: <count>
- unique_mss_values: <comma-separated sorted list of distinct MSS values>
- unique_window_scale_values: <comma-separated sorted list of distinct window scale shift values>
- sack_permitted_percentage: <N>% (rounded to nearest integer)
- timestamp_present_percentage: <N>% (rounded to nearest integer)
- most_common_mss: <value>
- most_common_dst_port: <port>
